bullets: Sabic late-June offers high: yarn 1,500, copo 1,550-1,600, random 1,640 CIF.
IRPC back after 2 months: yarn 1,390, 1126NK 1,480 CFR HCMC.
Marubeni NSRP 114G to 36,500; market sellers down to 36,200.
Sabic June PE down 50-300 vs last month: LDPE Film 1,650, HDPE Film 1,400.
Chinese coal-based LLDPE 7042 to 34,700; oil-based matching coal lows.
Sibur LLDPE Film to 35,300 (down 900) but still above Chinese.
Sellers cut coal-based SG-5 to 22,000-22,100 to compete.
Oil-based pushed below 25,000; Sinopec, Shintech among cuts.
Formosa re-offer 30-40 lower (930 CIF) still unaccepted.

editorial.changedNote: Mixed; PVC coal-based and a few PP/PE items eased

summary: Sabic announced June PE offers to Vietnam, down 50-300 vs last month across grades.
Chinese-origin PE stayed abundant with prices flat to slightly lower; some oil-based LLDPE matched coal lows.
Sibur cut PE/PP at Hai Phong but stayed above competitive Chinese imports.
paras: On May 26, Sabic PE offers were seen as more reasonable than its PP list: LDPE Film 1,650 CIF, LDPE coating 1,800, LLDPE Film 1,300-1,310, mLLDPE C6 1,700, HDPE Film 1,400, HDPE blow 1,450, CIF ex-Singapore - down 50-300 vs last month.

Chinese PE stayed plentiful, early-week prices flat to soft: Ningmei coal-based 7042 at 1,180 CIF, Shenhua 7042/7050 at 1,220; Sabic-Tianjin oil-based 222WT at 1,270; Petrochina 7042/7042N at 1,180 like coal goods; BASF China HDPE 6095 at 1,350.
Sibur offered at Hai Phong in VND: LLDPE Film 35,300 (down 900), mLLDPE 38,200, HDPE Film 37,800-38,000 (down 500-700), HDPE blow 37,000, HDPE injection 36,500-37,000.
Even Sibur LLDPE was not cheapest: at least two distributors cut Chinese oil-based 7042/7042N to 34,700 for late-June delivery.
HDPE sellers cut deeply - Equate 6888 and Qamar F1 to 41,000 (~2,000 lower), some at 40,500 to clear early; most HDPE yarn below 43,000.
LDPE kept falling: Lotte Titan 260GG from a 51,000 low to 50,000, with one seller at 49,000.

summary: Sabic offered surprisingly high PP for late-June (yarn 1,500, copo 1,550-1,600, random 1,640 CIF).
Thailand IRPC resumed PP/PS offers after a 2-month break, signaling recovering supply.
Domestic pressure persisted from NSRP spot sales and distributor cuts.
paras: On May 26, Sabic offered late-June PP from Saudi and Korean plants: yarn/injection 1,500 CIF, copo 1,550-1,600, random 1,640 - seen as too high.
IRPC returned after 2 months with a full PP range: yarn/injection 1,390 CFR HCMC, PP Film 1126NK 1,480, 1100RC 1,430, B1101 extrusion 1,510 - still high but a sign supply is recovering.

Jinneng PP yarn HP550J/HP500N to Vietnam at 1,290 CIF (lower than last week), BOPP at 1,300; Dongguan BOPP up to 1,410, PP Film 1,460, Fibre 1,410; Oriental Fibre 1,390; Petrochina L5E89/T30S at 1,250.
Sibur PP homo at 36,000-36,300 (down 1,000 vs early last week); non-woven PP at 36,600.
Marubeni offered NSRP 114G at 36,500 (500 lower); market sellers down to 36,200. NSRP readied a 27,000-ton 114G/105G June spot sale, pressuring supply.
In the South, BSR T3034 offered at 36,800 (down 700). Other domestic PP unchanged; merchant offers drifted sideways to slightly lower on competition.

summary: Sellers cut Chinese coal-based SG-5 to 22,000-22,100, pushing buyers to caution.
Coal-based cuts dragged oil-based below 25,000 for Chinese and Shintech goods.
AGC/TPC Vina held June offers; Formosa re-offer 30-40 lower still unaccepted.
paras: On May 25, a northern distributor offered coal-based SG-5 Erdos at 22,500 (down 500); one buyer signed at 22,100 at Yen Vien, a market low.
On May 26, other sellers cut Chinese SG-5 to 22,000-22,100 to compete. Continuous self-cutting kept sentiment cautious; buyers signed only in moderation.
Falling coal prices pressured oil-based PVC: more suppliers offered Chinese oil-based and Shintech SE950 below 25,000.
AGC Vina and TPC Vina held June SP660/SG660/PM66R at 22,800-23,100 ex-VAT (~24,624-24,948 with VAT), focusing on closing orders.
Sinopec S1000 had dropped to 24,600 with few trades; buyers bid below 23,000.
Formosa re-offered June-July 30-40 lower: S65/S65D/S60/S70 and B57 at 930 CIF, S57 at 940, 5% tax - buyers still not ready.
